title: Rooms: Kitchen
question: Where do you cook food?
explanation: The kitchen is the room where you prepare and cook meals.

title: Rooms: Bedroom
question: Where do you sleep at night?
explanation: The bedroom is the room with your bed, where you sleep.

title: Rooms: Bathroom
question: Where do you take a shower?
explanation: The bathroom has a shower, a sink, and a toilet.

title: Rooms: Living Room
question: Where does the family sit together to watch TV?
explanation: The living room is where the family relaxes together.

title: Rooms: Dining Room
question: Where does the family eat dinner together?
explanation: The dining room has a big table where the family eats meals.

title: Furniture: Chair
question: What piece of furniture has four legs and a back, made for one person to sit on?
explanation: A chair is a piece of furniture you sit on, with a back and usually four legs.

title: Furniture: Table
question: Where do you put your plate when you eat?
explanation: A table is a flat surface where you put things, like food.

title: Furniture: Bed
question: What do you sleep on?
explanation: A bed is the furniture in your bedroom where you sleep.

title: Furniture: Sofa
question: What is a long, soft seat for two or three people?
explanation: A sofa (or couch) is big and soft, and more than one person can sit on it.

title: Furniture: Desk
question: Where do you sit to write or use a computer?
explanation: A desk is a table used for working, writing, or studying.

title: Furniture: Shelf
question: Where do you put books on the wall?
explanation: A shelf is a flat board on the wall where you store books or objects.

title: Family: Mother
question: Who is your female parent?
explanation: Your mother is the woman who is your parent.

title: Family: Father
question: Who is your male parent?
explanation: Your father is the man who is your parent.

title: Family: Sister
question: What do you call the girl who has the same parents as you?
explanation: Your sister is a girl or woman who has the same parents as you.

title: Family: Brother
question: What do you call the boy who has the same parents as you?
explanation: Your brother is a boy or man who has the same parents as you.

title: Family: Grandmother
question: Who is your mother's mother?
explanation: Your grandmother is the mother of your mother or father.

title: Family: Grandfather
question: Who is your father's father?
explanation: Your grandfather is the father of your mother or father.

title: Family: Son
question: What do parents call their male child?
explanation: A son is a boy or man in relation to his parents.

title: Family: Daughter
question: What do parents call their female child?
explanation: A daughter is a girl or woman in relation to her parents.

title: Family: Uncle
question: Who is your mother's brother?
explanation: Your uncle is the brother of your mother or father.

title: Body: Head
question: What is the top part of your body?
explanation: Your head is at the top. It has your face, eyes, and brain.

title: Body: Arm
question: What body part connects your shoulder to your hand?
explanation: Your arm goes from your shoulder down to your hand.

title: Body: Hand
question: What do you use to hold things?
explanation: Your hand is at the end of your arm. It has five fingers.

title: Body: Eye
question: What do you see with?
explanation: Your eyes let you see the world around you.

title: Body: Ear
question: What do you hear with?
explanation: Your ears are on the sides of your head. You use them to hear.

title: Body: Nose
question: What do you smell with?
explanation: Your nose is in the middle of your face. You use it to smell.

title: Body: Mouth
question: What do you eat and talk with?
explanation: Your mouth is on your face. You use it to eat food and to speak.

title: Body: Leg
question: What body part do you use to walk and kick?
explanation: Your leg is the long body part between your hip and your foot.

title: Body: Foot
question: What is at the bottom of your leg?
explanation: Your foot is at the end of your leg. You stand on your feet.

title: Body: Finger
question: What are the five small parts at the end of your hand?
explanation: Fingers are the five thin parts on each hand.

title: Home: Door
question: What do you open to enter a room?
explanation: A door opens and closes so you can go in and out of a room.

title: Home: Window
question: What do you look through to see outside?
explanation: A window is made of glass. You can see outside through it.

title: Home: Lamp
question: What gives light in a dark room?
explanation: A lamp makes light so you can see when it is dark.

title: Home: Wall
question: What are the flat sides of a room?
explanation: Walls are the vertical surfaces around a room.

title: Home: Floor
question: What do you walk on inside a room?
explanation: The floor is the flat surface at the bottom of a room.

title: Home: Roof
question: What is on top of a house?
explanation: The roof covers the top of a house and keeps rain out.

title: Descriptions: Tall
question: How do you describe a person who is NOT short?
explanation: A tall person has a large height.

title: Descriptions: Short
question: How do you describe a person who is NOT tall?
explanation: A short person has a small height.

title: Descriptions: Old
question: How do you describe a person who is NOT young?
explanation: An old person has lived many years.

title: Descriptions: Young
question: How do you describe a child?
explanation: A young person has not lived many years.

title: Descriptions: Hair
question: What grows on top of your head?
explanation: Hair grows on your head. It can be long or short.

title: Family Context
question: A girl with the same mother and father as me is my ___.
explanation: A girl who shares your parents is your sister.

title: Body Context
question: I wear a hat on my ___.
explanation: A hat goes on your head.

title: Home Context
question: I turn on the ___ when the room is dark.
explanation: You turn on a lamp to make light in a dark room.

title: Furniture Context
question: I sit on a ___ at the dining table.
explanation: You sit on a chair when you eat at the table.

title: Room Context
question: The fridge is in the ___.
explanation: A fridge keeps food cold and is found in the kitchen.

title: Family Context 2
question: My father's mother is my ___.
explanation: Your father's mother is your grandmother.

title: Body Context 2
question: I write with my ___.
explanation: You hold a pen in your hand to write.

title: Description Context
question: A person who is 90 years old is very ___.
explanation: Someone who has lived 90 years is old.

title: Home Context 2
question: I look through the ___ to see the garden.
explanation: A window is made of glass, and you can see outside through it.
